title: thymus pulegioides subsp. carniolicus
intro: Thymus pulegioides subsp. carniolicus, also known as alpine thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It possesses medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and to aid in digestion. The health benefits of alpine thyme include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation in the body. Alpine thyme can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or added to topical creams and salves, allowing for a variety of applications in traditional medicine.
